old_claim.note: Nanotechnology (Early Research) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because atomic layer deposition emerged as a thin-film deposition technique before nanotechnology was named as a field.

source_shape.source_locator: Review discussion of atomic layer deposition history and its relationship to nanoscale thin-film manufacturing.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review treats ALD as a deposition method with roots in earlier thin-film chemistry rather than as a technology caused by the nanotechnology field label.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: ALD is nanoscale in use, but the dependency should point to CVD/thin-film chemistry; making it depend on nanotechnology reverses the chronology and overuses a broad field label.

ontology_before: ALD carried a historical predecessor edge to a later field label, creating a false prerequisite after nanotechnology was dated to 1974.
ontology_after: ALD now depends on chemical vapor deposition, which is the more concrete upstream process lineage already present in the graph.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The ALD node were narrowed to mean only post-1974 nanotechnology-branded ALD applications.
A stronger source showed the specific ALD process in this graph did not exist until after nanotechnology terminology shaped it.
The graph added a more precise nanoscale metrology node that is actually prerequisite to this ALD scope.
short_reason: ALD should not depend on the later nanotechnology field label.
